notes.total: 103 tickets delivered across 6 sprints represents a strong throughput at ~1.1 tickets/day, indicating healthy team capacity and scope management.
notes.completed: Perfect 100% completion rate with all 103 tickets closed demonstrates exceptional quarter execution and planning discipline.
notes.completion_rate: 100% completion achieved — team met all quarter commitments with zero carryover, showcasing excellent predictability and follow-through.
notes.releases_shipped: 10 releases shipped across the quarter (median 2 per sprint) demonstrates consistent delivery cadence and strong deployment discipline.
notes.oos_total: Zero out-of-sprint additions — sprint planning was highly disciplined with no reactive work disrupting committed scope.
notes.oos_open: No outstanding out-of-sprint items — all planned work completed as intended with no unplanned debt.
notes.type_split: 26% bugs (27 of 103) is within acceptable range but warrants monitoring — majority focus on stories (61) and tasks (15) indicates healthy forward progress.
notes.avg_releases: Median of 2 releases per sprint (average 1.7) shows consistent shipping rhythm with most sprints delivering multiple releases.
notes.time_logged: 761.5 hours logged against 1,482 estimated represents significant under-logging at 51% — either estimates are inflated or time tracking needs improvement.
notes.estimate_accuracy: 49% variance (actual time half of estimates) indicates systematic over-estimation — team should recalibrate story pointing or improve logging discipline.
notes.no_estimate: 6 tickets (6%) completed without estimates is excellent hygiene — planning data is reliable for nearly all work.
notes.rollover: Zero rollover tickets — team completed all committed work within their respective sprints, demonstrating strong velocity predictability.
notes.cycle_time: Median 8 days (average 10.8) shows good flow with some outliers — most tickets move efficiently with a few longer-running items pulling the mean up.
notes.assignee_workload: Work well-distributed: Giuseppe (32), Fabrizio (29), Nikola (23), Gianni (16) all at 100% completion with only 3 unassigned tickets — balanced load with no bottlenecks.
